items.title: Trung Quốc huy động 40.000 nhân công, đào đắp hơn 102 triệu m³ đất đá, tiêu tốn 983.798 tỷ để xây dựng công trình hạ tầng quy mô bậc nhất hành tinh, làm chậm cả tốc độ quay của Trái Đất
items.summary: Trung Quốc huy động 40.000 nhân công, đào đắp hơn 102 triệu m³ đất đá, tiêu tốn 983.798 tỷ để xây dựng công trình hạ tầng quy mô bậc nhất hành tinh, làm chậm cả tốc độ quay của Trái Đất nguoiquansat.vn

items.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
items.summary: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

items.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
items.summary: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

items.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
items.summary: Companies added slightly fewer workers than forecast, with hiring targeted heavily toward healthcare-related sectors.

items.title: North Sea oil and renewables: The UK’s next PM faces a defining energy policy decision
items.summary: Andy Burnham could face an early test over North Sea oil drilling as energy shocks revive the UK energy security debate.

items.title: Do you really, really love your job? Then you're not alone, according to surprising results from this survey
items.summary: Those surveyed showed a 78.9% rate of workers who "reported feeling positive at the end of their shifts."

items.title: China widens Japan export curbs, targeting drone makers, nuclear firms and defense institutes
items.summary: China blacklisted four of Japan's government defense research institutes and placed dozens more Japanese firms under tightened export restrictions Monday.

items.title: Shipping rebounds in Strait of Hormuz one week after U.S.-Iran deal – but fragile confidence threatens recovery
items.summary: Shipping traffic through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz is recovering one week after the U.S. and Iran signed an interim peace deal.

items.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
items.summary: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.

items.title: Kevin Warsh names members of his Federal Reserve task forces, including Marc Andreessen, Doug McMillon
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh on Thursday released names of the experts who will comprise five task forces to examine the institution's operations.

items.title: Prediction markets spark insider trading concerns. Here's how Goldman and other companies are responding
items.summary: CNBC reached out to 50 companies about what are their trading policies for employees on prediction markets. A handful had an answer.

items.title: Kalshi traders think gas prices will stay higher for longer as U.S.-Iran tensions heat back up
items.summary: Odds that gas prices are above $3.50 on Election Day have jumped to 75% on the platform.

items.title: Goldman Sachs wins $70 billion in asset management deals with Verizon, Lockheed Martin
items.summary: Competition in the multitrillion-dollar market for retirement assets is fierce among managers such as Goldman Sachs, BlackRock, Russell Investments and Mercer.

items.title: Michael Burry bets on sportsbooks DraftKings and Flutter, sees prediction markets curbed by regulation
items.summary: The investor believes regulators will eventually crack down on prediction markets after competition from the upstarts pressured the stocks of sportsbooks.

items.title: Fed officials were split on direction of interest rates at last meeting, minutes show
items.summary: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released minutes from its June 16-17 meeting.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal until 2027 after latest setback, Kalshi traders predict
items.summary: Speculators now see just a 43% chance that traffic flows return to normal by Dec. 1.

items.title: Lorde says Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
items.summary: Lorde was performing at the Real Cool Festival in Madrid on Thursday and took some time during her set to speak out against AI glasses. While she didn't specify any brands in particular, it's likely she was taking a shot at festival sponsor Ray-Ban, which has collaborated with Meta on a pair of AI smartglasses. […]

items.title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
items.summary: OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave of news about ChatGPT Work today, the company confirmed that it will be "sunsetting" Atlas and is targeting an […]

items.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
items.summary: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

items.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
items.summary: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.
